Class TestPlatform

java.lang.Object
dev.ua.theroer.magicutils.diagnostics.testkit.TestPlatform
All Implemented Interfaces:
dev.ua.theroer.magicutils.platform.ConfigFormatProvider, dev.ua.theroer.magicutils.platform.Platform
public final class TestPlatform extends Object implements dev.ua.theroer.magicutils.platform.Platform, dev.ua.theroer.magicutils.platform.ConfigFormatProvider
In-memory Platform implementation for JUnit-style tests that need to run diagnostics without a real Minecraft server.

The platform is deliberately minimal and platform-agnostic: it exposes a caller-provided config directory, a NoOpPlatformLogger, no console or player audiences, and a single-thread TaskScheduler. runOnMain(Runnable) runs work inline on the calling thread and isMainThread() always reports true, which keeps synchronous diagnostics checks deterministic.

It also implements ConfigFormatProvider, defaulting to the json config extension so that ConfigManager produces stable, human-readable fixtures under the temporary directory.

Prefer DiagnosticsTestHarness, which wires this platform together with a ConfigManager, LoggerCore and MagicRuntime. Use TestPlatform directly only when you need finer control over the wiring. Callers that construct it directly must invoke shutdown() to release the backing scheduler thread.

  • Constructor Details

    • TestPlatform

      public TestPlatform(Path configDir)
      Creates a test platform rooted at the given config directory.
      Parameters:
      configDir - base directory for configs/lang files (typically a JUnit @TempDir); must not be null

    • shutdown

      public void shutdown()
      Shuts down the backing task scheduler. Safe to call multiple times.

      DiagnosticsTestHarness.close() calls this automatically; only invoke it manually when you construct the platform yourself.
